User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the Turnos Manzano application.

Appointment.title: Appointment
Appointment.description: Represents an appointment for a museum visit.

startTime.description: The start time of the appointment.

endTime.description: The end time of the appointment.

ScheduleConfiguration.title: ScheduleConfiguration
ScheduleConfiguration.description: Represents the configuration for available appointment days and times.

id.description: Unique identifier for the schedule configuration.

dayOfWeek.description: The day of the week for the configuration (e.g., Monday, Tuesday).

startTime.description: The start time for available appointments on this day.

endTime.description: The end time for available appointments on this day.

definition.description: Stores user profiles. Path-based ownership enables straightforward security rules based on `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

definition.description: Stores appointments associated with a specific user.  Path-based ownership (user ID in the path) ensures that only the user can access their appointments.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: appointmentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the appointment.

definition.description: Stores configurations for available appointment days and times. Access should be restricted to administrators.

params.name: scheduleConfigurationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the schedule configuration.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to ensure Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, and support secure list operations (QAPs). It leverages path-based ownership for user data and schedule configurations, while appointments are structured under the respective user to ensure clear ownership and simplified security rules. 

Authorization Independence:

*   The `/users/{userId}/appointments/{appointmentId}` path establishes a clear ownership model where appointments are directly associated with users, eliminating the need to `get()` data from other collections to validate ownership.

QAPs Support:

*   The segregation of user-owned data under `/users/{userId}` allows for efficient and secure `list` operations scoped to individual users, without exposing data from other users. Schedule configurations are global, so rules can enforce only admin writes.
